{{Quote|Do not see a lightsaber duel as a choice between winning and losing. Every duel can have many, many outcomes. When you concentrate solely on winning—in lightsaber duels as in everything else—you sully your victory. Winning becomes worse than losing. It is better to lose well than to win badly. And it is always better to end a duel peacefully than to win or lose.|Repka De|<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
'''Rekpa De''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Repka De was [[Jedi training/Legends|instructed]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Trained as a [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Guardian]], Master De was well-trained in the art of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]]; so much so that he was selected by the acting [[Battlemaster/Legends|battlemaster]] to gain the title of [[Jedi instructor|lightsaber instructor]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. After the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] approved the appointment, Master De began teaching basic lightsaber techniques at the Temple to the many [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] that passed through the [[Jedi academy|Temple academy]]. Master De, an instructor while the famed Jedi [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] was still a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knight]] himself, was often quoted in regards to his views on winning and losing.<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)" />
